subject: [PATCH v4 0/4] arm64: cross-CPU NMI via SDEI

reviews.author: Doug Anderson
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Doug Anderson noted that the patch does not handle the case where SDEI is disabled, which would prevent the cross-CPU NMI mechanism from working.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Hi,

On Wed, Jun 17, 2026 at 12:20 PM Kiryl Shutsemau <kirill@shutemov.name> wrote:

reviews.author: Doug Anderson
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Doug Anderson pointed out that the patch does not provide a clear explanation of how the SDEI event is delivered to the target CPU, and requested more information on this critical aspect of the mechanism.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Hi,

On Wed, Jun 17, 2026 at 12:20 PM Kiryl Shutsemau <kirill@shutemov.name> wrote:

2026-06-17.patch_summary: This patch series adds a new mechanism to deliver NMI (Non-Maskable Interrupt) across CPUs on arm64 architecture using SDEI (System Description Event Infrastructure). This is necessary for certain debug and observability features, such as capturing CPU backtraces and crash dumps, which require interrupting a CPU that has its interrupts locally masked. The existing pseudo-NMI mechanism is too expensive in terms of performance overhead, so this new approach uses SDEI to deliver the NMI at a lower cost. This allows for more accurate debugging and observability features without introducing significant performance regressions.
